claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 4 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information to assess your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Remember, Child Benefit eligibility is determined on a per-child basis, so we'll need to discuss each child individually.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. Her name is Alex and she's 4 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 4 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, she potentially qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes. Now I need to ask you some questions about your relationship and living arrangements with Alex.

**Are you Alex's parent, or do you have parental responsibility for her?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living arrangements:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to understand your UK connection:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently in full-time work (meaning paid work for 24 hours or more per week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's only 4, so she's not working at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course, that makes sense! 

Now, one more important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me review Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:

✓ Alex is 4 years old (under 16, so she qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes)
✓ You are her parent with parental responsibility
✓ Alex lives with you
✓ You live in the UK (Manchester)
✓ Alex is not in full-time work
✓ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key criteria:
- You're responsible for a qualifying child (Alex, who is under 16)
- She lives with you
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming for her

**Important note about the High Income Child Benefit Charge:**
If you or your partner individually earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge through your tax return. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit even if you earn above this threshold.

Would you like information about how to make your claim, or do you have any other questions?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great news, thank you so much for your help! I understand that I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. I think I have all the information I need for now.
